zh.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine是Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton和Joan de  Basing的兒子，出生在英國肯特書爾斯特。他於大約1369年與Margaret de Vere在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1375年9月24日在英國米德萊可波普拉爾，埋葬在英國米德萊可堂爾 迪維希。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine是Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton和Joan de  Basing的儿子，出生在英国肯特書爾斯特。他于大约1369年与Margaret de Vere在英国肯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1375年9月24日在英国米德萊可波普拉爾，埋葬在英国米德萊可堂爾 迪維希。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lovaine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Perrine, Pierre.

French and English: habitational name from Lorraine, a region in the northeastern part of France. Its name derives from the name of the medieval kingdom of Lotharingia, which in turn was named for its sovereign Lothar (a personal name composed of the elements hlūd ‘famous, renowned’ + hari, heri ‘army’).
Altered form of French   Lorrain  , a variant of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine was born in Penshurst, Kent, England as the son of Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton and Joan de  Basing. He married Margaret de Vere about 1369, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 24 September 1375, in Poplar, Middlesex, England, and was buried in Tower Division, Middlesex, England.

fr.Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Eng: Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine est né à Penshurst, Kent, Angleterre, fils de Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton et Joan de  Basing. Il a épousé Margaret de Vere environ 1369, à Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1375, à Poplar, Middlesex, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Tower Division, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Eng: Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, England
de.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine wurde in Penshurst, Kent, England, als Sohn von Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton und Joan de  Basing geboren. Er hat um 1369 in Kent, England Margaret de Vere geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 24. September 1375 in Poplar, Middlesex, England und wurde in Tower Division, Middlesex, England begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret de Vere circa 1369, nel Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas de LovaineはイギリスケントペンシュルストでThomas de Louvain Lord of Little EastonとJoan de  Basingの息子として生まれた。約1369年にイギリスケントでMargaret de Vereと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1375年9月24日にイギリスミッドレセックスポプラーで死去し，イギリスミッドレセックストウエル ディヴィシオンにて埋葬された。

ko.Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Eng: 영국 더비실르 더비 더비 스트 알크먼드
ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine(은)는 영국 켄트 펜슈르스트에서 Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton(와)과 Joan de  Basing의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1369년 영국 켄트에서 Margaret de Vere(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1375년 9월 24일 영국 미들레세스 포플라에서 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 타워 디비시언에 묻혔다.

pt.Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Eng: Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine nasceu em Penshurst, Kent, Inglaterra filho de Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton e Joan de  Basing. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Vere aproximadamente 1369, em Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de setembro de 1375, em Poplar, Middlesex, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Tower Division, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.Derby St Alkmund, Derby, Derbyshire, Eng: дирби ст алкмунд, дирби, дирбишир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Lovaine родился в местности пеншурст, кэнт, Англия. Его родителей звали Thomas de Louvain Lord of Little Easton и Joan de  Basing. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Vere около 1369 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 24 сентября 1375 г. в местности поплар, миддлесекс, Англия и был похоронен в местности тоуир дивисион, миддлесекс, Англия.
